title: Calibrating the Camera to Subject Distance
description:
Calibrating the Camera to Subject Distance

Calibrating the Camera to Subject Distance

For optimal searching results, the size of the features in an image should be the same at search time as it was when the model was taught.  Assuming the same camera and lens are used, if the camera to subject distance changes between the time the model is trained and the time the search is performed, the features in the search window

will have a different apparent size.  That is, if the camera is closer to the features they will appear larger; if the camera is farther away they will appear smaller.

Note:  If the camera to subject distance changes, you must retrain the model.
